cards.title: Expert Team
cards.description: Your journey is guided by board-certified fertility specialists, experienced IVF coordinators, licensed legal advisors, and dedicated patient care managers. Our multidisciplinary team works together so medical, legal, and logistical decisions are never made in isolation giving you confidence that every detail is handled by a qualified professional.

cards.title: Comprehensive Care
cards.description: From your first free consultation through embryo transfer, pregnancy monitoring, birth, and post-birth documentation, we manage the entire process under one roof. You receive regular medical updates, coordinated clinic appointments, legal contract preparation, and embassy support so you never have to chase answers across multiple providers.

cards.title: Personalized Approach
cards.description: Every family's medical history, timeline, budget, and legal requirements are different. We offer tailored programs including surrogacy with own gametes, surrogacy with an egg donor, egg freezing, and VIP concierge services. Your coordinator builds a custom plan around your goals rather than forcing you into a one-size-fits-all package.

cards.title: Global Network
cards.description: We serve intended parents from the United States, United Kingdom, Israel, China, Russia, Spain, and across Europe and Asia. Our team provides translation support, travel coordination, accommodation guidance, and embassy liaison services removing language and logistics barriers so you can focus on your growing family.

cards.title: 24/7 Support
cards.description: Surrogacy journeys do not follow office hours. Whether you have an urgent medical question, need a document translated overnight, or want a pregnancy update across time zones, your dedicated coordinator and our support team are available around the clock. You are never left waiting when something matters.

guide.title: How to Choose a Surrogacy Agency
guide.subtitle: Not all agencies offer the same level of medical oversight, legal protection, or ongoing support. Use this checklist to evaluate any agency and see how Happy Family meets each standard.

criteria.title: Medical Oversight & Clinic Partnerships
criteria.description: A reputable surrogacy agency works directly with accredited fertility clinics, not independent brokers with no clinical involvement. Verify that the agency coordinates IVF procedures, embryo transfers, and prenatal care through licensed medical facilities with published success rates.
questions: Which IVF clinics does the agency partner with?
Who oversees the surrogate's prenatal care?
Can you speak directly with the medical team?

criteria.title: Legal Framework & Parentage Protection
criteria.description: Your parental rights must be secured before treatment begins. In Georgia, intended parents are recognized on the birth certificate from birth under Article 143 of the Law on Health Care. Confirm the agency provides notarized contracts, legal counsel, and post-birth documentation support for your home country.
questions: Are parental rights established before embryo transfer?
Does the agency provide legal counsel familiar with Georgian law?
What is the birth certificate and passport exit process?

criteria.title: Transparent Pricing
criteria.description: Surrogacy is a significant financial commitment. The best agencies provide itemized, all-inclusive budgets with no hidden fees. Ask what is covered surrogate compensation, medical procedures, legal fees, insurance, and travel and whether fixed-fee packages are available.
questions: Is there a written, itemized cost breakdown?
What costs are not included in the quoted price?

criteria.title: Rigorous Screening Standards
criteria.description: Thorough screening of surrogates and intended parents protects everyone involved. Medical evaluations, psychological assessments, and background checks should be standard not optional add-ons. Ask about disqualification criteria and how long screening typically takes.
questions: What medical and psychological tests do surrogates undergo?
How are intended parents screened for eligibility?
How long does the matching process take after screening?

criteria.title: Emotional Support & Counselling
criteria.description: Surrogacy is emotionally demanding for intended parents and surrogates alike. Look for agencies that offer counselling sessions, peer community connections, and a dedicated coordinator who knows your case personally not a call center that routes you to a different person each time.
questions: Is psychological counselling included in the program?
Will I have one dedicated coordinator throughout?
What support is available during the pregnancy?

criteria.title: Track Record & Credentials
criteria.description: Verify the agency's history, success metrics, and professional affiliations. Ask how long they have operated, how many families they have served, and whether you can speak with former clients. Ethical agencies welcome transparency about their credentials and outcomes.
questions: How many years has the agency been operating?
Can you provide references from former intended parents?
What professional certifications does the team hold?

georgia.title: Why Choose a Surrogacy Agency in Georgia
georgia.subtitle: Georgia has been a leading international surrogacy destination since 2011 offering legal clarity, affordable programs, and world-class fertility care in Tbilisi.
georgia.overview: For intended parents comparing options in the United States, United Kingdom, or other countries, Georgia offers a uniquely favorable combination: clear legislation protecting intended parents, significantly lower program costs, accredited IVF clinics, and a streamlined birth certificate process that typically takes just 5–10 days. Happy Family has specialized in Georgia surrogacy for international families since 2009, giving us deep expertise in both Georgian law and the exit requirements of dozens of home countries.

points.title: Legal Protection Since 2011
points.description: Georgian law explicitly recognizes intended parents as the sole legal parents from birth. The surrogate has no parental rights, and your names appear directly on the birth certificate.

points.title: Up to 70% Cost Savings
points.description: All-inclusive surrogacy programs in Georgia start from approximately $40,000 a fraction of typical US surrogacy costs with transparent, itemized pricing and no hidden fees.

points.title: Fast Exit Process
points.description: No court orders or post-birth adoption proceedings are required. After birth, the Civil Registry issues your baby's birth certificate within days, and we assist with embassy documentation for your return home.

points.title: Accredited Tbilisi Clinics
points.description: IVF procedures, embryo transfers, and deliveries take place at modern, accredited facilities in Tbilisi with experienced reproductive medicine specialists and high clinical standards.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to the questions intended parents ask most when choosing a surrogacy agency.

items.question: How do I choose a surrogacy agency?
items.answer: Evaluate agencies on six key criteria: medical oversight and clinic partnerships, legal parentage protection, transparent pricing, rigorous screening standards, emotional support and counselling, and a verifiable track record. Request a written cost breakdown, ask to speak with former clients, and confirm that legal contracts are prepared before any medical procedures begin.

items.question: What should I ask a surrogacy agency before signing?
items.answer: Ask which clinics perform IVF and deliveries, how parental rights are legally secured, what the total all-inclusive cost covers, how surrogates are medically and psychologically screened, who your dedicated coordinator will be, and what post-birth support is included for embassy documentation and travel home.

items.question: How do I verify a surrogacy agency's credentials?
items.answer: Check how long the agency has operated, request references from completed families, verify partnerships with accredited clinics, confirm legal counsel is licensed and experienced in Georgian surrogacy law, and review whether success metrics and program details are published transparently on their website.

items.question: Why choose an agency in Georgia over the US or UK?
items.answer: Georgia offers legally protected gestational surrogacy since 2011, with intended parents named on the birth certificate at birth. Programs cost up to 70% less than US alternatives, IVF clinics in Tbilisi meet international standards, and the exit process requires no court orders or adoption proceedings making it especially attractive for international intended parents.

items.question: What makes Happy Family different from other agencies?
items.answer: Happy Family combines 15+ years of international experience, in-house medical and legal coordination, a dedicated coordinator for every family, transparent fixed-fee packages, rigorous surrogate screening, 24/7 multilingual support, and specialized expertise in embassy exit documentation for parents returning to the US, UK, Israel, China, and across Europe.

items.question: Is surrogacy legal in Georgia for foreigners?
items.answer: Yes. Gestational surrogacy has been legal in Georgia since 2011 and is available to international intended parents who meet eligibility requirements, including heterosexual married or cohabiting couples with documented medical need. Happy Family coordinates all legal contracts and birth certificate registration in compliance with Georgian law.

items.question: What support do intended parents receive during the journey?
items.answer: Every intended parent is assigned a dedicated coordinator who manages clinic appointments, legal contracts, surrogate matching, pregnancy updates, and post-birth documentation. We also provide emotional counselling, financial planning guidance, translation services, travel coordination, and 24/7 urgent support throughout the entire process.

items.question: How long does the surrogacy process take with Happy Family?
items.answer: Most journeys take 12–18 months from initial consultation to birth, depending on surrogate matching, medical screening, legal preparation, and IVF outcomes. We provide a realistic timeline during your free consultation based on your specific program and medical profile.

hero.title: The Surrogacy Process in Georgia Country
hero.description: From your first consultation in Tbilisi to birth certificate registration, Happy Family guides international intended parents through every stage of gestational surrogacy in Georgia. Our step-by-step program covers IVF coordination, surrogate matching, legal contracts under Georgia surrogacy laws, pregnancy monitoring, and post-birth support for families worldwide.

process.title: How Gestational Surrogacy Works in Georgia
process.subtitle: A clear roadmap for international families pursuing surrogacy in Tbilisi, from onboarding to bringing your baby home.

steps.title: Initial Consultation & Program Selection
steps.description: Meet your coordinator in Tbilisi or via video call to discuss your fertility goals, surrogacy cost in Georgia, and program options. We review your medical history, explain gestational surrogacy laws in Georgia country, and recommend the right pathway with own gametes, egg donation, or embryo shipping before you commit to a Georgia surrogacy program.

steps.title: Legal Contracts & Screening
steps.description: Licensed Georgian counsel drafts notarized surrogacy contracts that secure your parental rights under Article 143 of Georgia's Health Care Law. Intended parents complete parent screening while your matched surrogate undergoes medical, psychological, and background evaluation at accredited clinics in Tbilisi. This dual screening protects everyone before IVF begins.

steps.title: IVF, Matching & Embryo Transfer
steps.description: Our team coordinates IVF at partner fertility clinics in Tbilisi, including egg donation if needed, and carefully matches you with a screened gestational surrogate. Embryos are created through IVF, quality-tested, and transferred to the surrogate. Your case manager handles scheduling, communication, and medical updates throughout the gestational surrogacy process in Georgia.

steps.title: Pregnancy, Birth & Registration
steps.description: The surrogate receives comprehensive prenatal care at modern maternity hospitals in Tbilisi while your coordinator keeps you informed at every milestone. At birth, intended parents are named directly on the birth certificate with no adoption required. We assist with Civil Registry documentation, passport application, and travel home so your Georgia surrogacy journey ends with your family united.

timeline.title: Surrogacy Timeline in Georgia
timeline.subtitle: Most international intended parents complete gestational surrogacy in Georgia country within 12 to 18 months. Exact timelines depend on IVF cycles, surrogate availability, and embryo shipping logistics.

phases.title: Months 1–3: Consultation, Contracts & Screening
phases.description: Initial consultation, program selection, legal contract signing, and completion of parent and surrogate screening. IVF or embryo preparation begins at clinics in Tbilisi once all parties are medically and legally cleared for surrogacy in Georgia.

phases.title: Months 3–6: IVF Cycles & Embryo Transfer
phases.description: Egg retrieval, fertilization, and embryo transfer to your matched surrogate. If using donor eggs or shipped embryos, this phase may include additional coordination. Pregnancy is confirmed through blood tests and early ultrasound at our partner clinics.

phases.title: Months 6–15: Pregnancy & Birth
phases.description: Regular prenatal monitoring, birth planning, and delivery at a Tbilisi maternity hospital. Birth certificate registration with intended parents' names typically completes within 5 to 10 days, followed by passport and travel arrangements for international families.

support.title: 24/7 Support Throughout Your Georgia Surrogacy Journey
support.subtitle: Every international family receives a personal case manager who coordinates clinics, legal counsel, surrogates, and logistics so you always have a single point of contact during gestational surrogacy in Georgia country.

cards.title: Personal Case Manager
cards.text: Your dedicated coordinator manages timelines, clinic appointments, and communication between intended parents, surrogates, and medical teams throughout every stage of surrogacy in Tbilisi.

cards.title: Medical & Legal Coordination
cards.text: We liaise with IVF clinics, maternity hospitals, and licensed Georgian attorneys to ensure contracts, screening, and treatment protocols meet Georgia surrogacy laws and international best practices.

cards.title: Post-Birth & Travel Support
cards.text: After delivery, we guide you through birth certificate registration, embassy documentation, and passport procedures so you can return home safely with your newborn.

faq.title: Surrogacy Process Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers for international intended parents about gestational surrogacy in Georgia country.

items.question: What is the surrogacy process in Georgia?
items.answer: The surrogacy process in Georgia includes consultation and program selection, legal contract signing, parent and surrogate screening, IVF and embryo transfer at clinics in Tbilisi, pregnancy monitoring, birth, and birth certificate registration. Happy Family manages every step for international intended parents pursuing gestational surrogacy in Georgia country.

items.question: What is gestational surrogacy in Georgia?
items.answer: Gestational surrogacy in Georgia means the surrogate carries an embryo created from the intended parents' or donors' gametes with no genetic link to the child. Under Georgian law since 2011, intended parents are recognized as the sole legal parents from birth, with their names on the birth certificate.

items.question: How long does the surrogacy process take in Georgia?
items.answer: Most gestational surrogacy programs in Georgia take 12 to 18 months from initial consultation to birth and registration. Timelines vary based on IVF success, surrogate matching, and whether egg donation or embryo shipping is required.

items.question: How much does surrogacy cost in Georgia?
items.answer: Gestational surrogacy in Georgia typically starts from around $40,000 with transparent all-inclusive packages covering IVF, surrogate compensation, legal services, and medical care. Costs depend on program type. Contact us for a personalized surrogacy cost quote tailored to your journey.

steps.title: Initial Consultation
steps.description: Meet your coordinator in Tbilisi or via video call to discuss your goals, timeline, surrogacy cost in Georgia, and program fit. We confirm basic eligibility for gestational surrogacy in Georgia country and outline the documents and medical records you will need.

steps.title: Medical & Psychological Review

faq.title: Intended Parent Screening Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers for international families about eligibility and screening for surrogacy in Georgia country.

items.question: What is intended parent screening for surrogacy in Georgia?
items.answer: Intended parent screening is the evaluation process international families complete before joining a gestational surrogacy program in Georgia. It includes a consultation, medical and fertility history review, psychological assessment, and legal eligibility check under Georgia surrogacy laws. Screening ensures the program is safe, ethical, and well-matched to your needs.

items.question: What documents are required for parent screening?
items.answer: Typically you will need passports, marriage or partnership documents (if applicable), medical records related to fertility, and identification for both intended parents. Your coordinator provides a tailored checklist based on your nationality and the legal requirements for surrogacy in Georgia country.

items.question: Is psychological screening required for surrogacy in Georgia country?
items.answer: Yes. Psychological screening is strongly recommended and often required for intended parents pursuing gestational surrogacy in Georgia. A brief assessment helps confirm you understand the emotional demands of international surrogacy and are prepared for the journey ahead.

items.question: How long does intended parent screening take?
items.answer: Most international intended parents complete screening within two to four weeks, depending on how quickly medical records and documents are gathered. Your coordinator in Tbilisi manages timelines and keeps you informed at each step of the Georgia surrogacy program onboarding process.

process.title: How Surrogate Screening Works in Georgia
process.subtitle: A step-by-step evaluation designed to protect surrogates, intended parents, and every child born through our gestational surrogacy program in Tbilisi.

steps.title: Medical Evaluation
steps.description: Comprehensive health checks, laboratory tests, and reproductive assessment at accredited IVF clinics in Tbilisi. We confirm each candidate meets surrogate requirements for gestational surrogacy in Georgia country  including prior healthy pregnancy history, BMI criteria, and overall reproductive fitness.

steps.title: Psychological Interview
steps.description: A licensed counsellor conducts a mental health screening and counselling session to confirm informed consent and emotional readiness. This step ensures every surrogate in our Georgia surrogacy program understands the commitment, risks, and support available throughout the journey.

steps.title: Social Background Check
steps.description: We review background history and verify home stability, including family support systems. This protects international intended parents and ensures surrogates in Tbilisi have the practical and emotional environment needed for a healthy gestational surrogacy pregnancy.

faq.title: Surrogate Screening Questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions about surrogate eligibility and screening for surrogacy in Georgia country.

items.question: What are the surrogate requirements in Georgia?
items.answer: Surrogate candidates must generally be healthy women aged 21–40 with at least one prior healthy pregnancy, non-smokers, and emotionally stable with a reliable support system. Full medical, psychological, and background screening at our Tbilisi clinics confirms eligibility for gestational surrogacy in Georgia country.

items.question: What medical tests do surrogates undergo?
items.answer: Surrogate screening includes blood work, infectious disease panels, reproductive health assessment, ultrasound evaluation, and a review of prior pregnancy history. All tests are performed at accredited IVF clinics in Tbilisi as part of our Georgia surrogacy program standards.

items.question: Why is psychological screening required for surrogates?
items.answer: Psychological screening confirms that each surrogate fully understands the gestational surrogacy process in Georgia, provides informed consent, and has the emotional resilience for the journey. It protects surrogates, intended parents, and the wellbeing of the child.

items.question: How long does surrogate screening take?
items.answer: Most surrogate candidates complete medical, psychological, and background screening within three to six weeks. Timelines depend on test scheduling at our Tbilisi clinics and the availability of prior medical records.

Thorough medical and genetic screening for safety
Clear scheduling and communication at every step

benefits.title: A Meaningful Gift, With Real Support

items.question: Is egg donation safe?
items.answer: Egg donation is generally safe when done under medical supervision. Our team monitors your health throughout and explains risks and expectations before you begin.

items.question: How long does the process take?
items.answer: Timelines vary, but most donors complete screening and a donation cycle over several weeks. We will provide a clear schedule based on your availability.

items.question: Can I stay anonymous?
items.answer: We follow strict privacy practices. The level of identity disclosure depends on legal and program requirements, which we will explain clearly during your consultation.

hero.title: Support & Counselling for Surrogacy in Georgia
hero.description: A surrogacy journey is as emotional as it is medical. Happy Family provides comprehensive support and counselling for international intended parents pursuing gestational surrogacy in Georgia country  from your first consultation in Tbilisi through birth and your return home. Your dedicated case manager coordinates medical updates, legal steps under Georgia surrogacy laws, and financial planning with transparent surrogacy cost guidance and 24/7 assistance.
cardPoints: Dedicated coordinator from consultation to birth
Licensed counsellors for individuals and couples

services.title: Comprehensive Support for Intended Parents
services.subtitle: Beyond matching and medical care  we guide international families through every practical, legal, and emotional step of surrogacy in Georgia.

cards.title: Emotional Support
cards.description: Compassionate counselling throughout your gestational surrogacy journey in Georgia. Our team understands the stress of fertility treatment, long-distance coordination, and waiting for milestones  and we are here for you at every turning point.

cards.title: Community Connection
cards.description: Connect with other international intended parents who understand the surrogacy process in Georgia country. Peer support, shared experiences, and a sense of community help you feel less alone during a journey that spans continents.

cards.title: Legal Guidance
cards.description: Clear explanation of Georgia surrogacy laws, contract preparation, and birth certificate registration. We coordinate with licensed legal counsel so intended parents understand parental rights, timelines, and post-birth documentation before treatment begins.

cards.title: Counselling Sessions
cards.description: One-on-one or couple sessions with qualified counsellors experienced in fertility and third-party reproduction. Sessions are available before, during, and after your Georgia surrogacy program to help you process decisions and manage stress.

cards.title: Financial Guidance
cards.description: Transparent surrogacy cost breakdowns, payment scheduling, and budgeting support for your program in Tbilisi. We explain what is included in all-inclusive packages, help you plan for contingencies, and answer questions about surrogacy cost in Georgia.

cards.title: 24/7 Case Management
cards.description: Round-the-clock assistance for urgent medical questions, pregnancy updates, document requests, and travel coordination. Your dedicated coordinator is reachable across time zones so international parents never wait when something matters.

journey.title: How We Support You Through Surrogacy in Georgia
journey.subtitle: Case management, counselling, and practical guidance from first inquiry to bringing your baby home.

steps.title: Pre-Program Consultation
steps.description: Your coordinator explains surrogacy program options in Tbilisi, Georgia surrogacy cost structures, and legal eligibility. Optional counselling sessions help you and your partner prepare emotionally before committing to a gestational surrogacy program.

steps.title: IVF, Matching & Legal Preparation
steps.description: We manage clinic scheduling, surrogate matching updates, and contract signing under Georgia surrogacy laws. Your coordinator keeps you informed at every milestone and connects you with counsellors if you need support during the wait.

steps.title: Pregnancy Monitoring & Updates
steps.description: Regular pregnancy reports, ultrasound summaries, and direct communication with your case manager. We coordinate translation, answer medical questions, and provide emotional support as your surrogate's pregnancy progresses in Tbilisi.

steps.title: Birth, Documentation & Homecoming
steps.description: Support through delivery, birth certificate registration, embassy documentation, and travel home. We guide international intended parents through the exit process with legal coordination and continued counselling as you transition to parenthood.

faq.title: Support & Counselling Questions

items.question: What support do intended parents receive during surrogacy in Georgia?
items.answer: Every intended parent is assigned a dedicated coordinator who manages clinic appointments, legal contracts, surrogate matching, pregnancy updates, and post-birth documentation. We also provide emotional counselling, financial planning guidance, translation services, travel coordination, and 24/7 urgent support throughout the entire gestational surrogacy process in Georgia.

items.question: Is counselling required for surrogacy in Georgia country?
items.answer: Psychological counselling is strongly recommended and often required as part of the screening process for both intended parents and surrogates. Happy Family offers licensed counselling sessions for individuals and couples to help you prepare for the emotional aspects of international surrogacy in Georgia.

items.question: How does case management work for international parents?
items.answer: Your case manager is your single point of contact from first consultation through birth. They coordinate with IVF clinics in Tbilisi, legal counsel, and the surrogate's care team  providing updates in your language, managing timelines, and handling urgent requests across time zones.

items.question: What financial guidance is included in a Georgia surrogacy program?
items.answer: We provide itemized surrogacy cost breakdowns, explain all-inclusive package inclusions, and help you plan payment schedules. Your coordinator answers questions about surrogacy cost in Georgia, contingency budgets, and what expenses to expect at each stage of the program.

cta.title: Ready for Personalised Support?
cta.description: Book a free consultation to meet your coordinator, discuss counselling options, and learn how we support international families through every step of surrogacy in Georgia country.

surrogacyWithOwnGametes.title: Surrogacy with Own Gametes
surrogacyWithOwnGametes.description: For couples who wish to maintain a biological connection with their child, surrogacy with own gametes offers the most natural path to parenthood. In this program, the intended mother's eggs and the intended father's sperm are used to create embryos through IVF, which are then transferred to a carefully screened surrogate.

ivfProcess.title: IVF Process
ivfProcess.step1: The IVF process begins with ovarian stimulation, egg retrieval, fertilization, and embryo culture in the lab. Fertilization can be done using conventional IVF or ICSI depending on sperm quality.
ivfProcess.step2: The process is carefully monitored to optimize egg quality and embryo development. Lab specialists assess embryos over several days to select the ones with the highest chance of implantation.
ivfProcess.step3: Each step is customized based on the medical profile of the intended parents, ensuring the approach is tailored to their specific needs and fertility factors.

embryoTransfer.title: Embryo Transfer
embryoTransfer.step1: After fertilization, the healthiest embryos are selected for transfer to the surrogate's uterus. Timing and preparation of the surrogate are carefully managed to maximize implantation success.
embryoTransfer.step2: The surrogate is monitored closely before, during, and after the transfer. Hormonal support and lifestyle guidance are provided to create the best environment for the embryo to thrive.
embryoTransfer.step3: Each step is customized based on the medical profile of the intended parents, ensuring the approach is tailored to their specific needs and fertility factors.

question1.q: What is the cost of surrogacy with own gametes?
question1.a: The surrogacy cost varies depending on several factors including medical procedures, surrogate compensation, legal fees, and program location. We provide a transparent surrogacy cost breakdown during your initial consultation. The surrogacy price includes all medical procedures, gestational surrogacy services, and comprehensive support throughout the journey.

question2.q: How much does surrogacy cost for couples?
question2.a: The cost of surrogacy for couples typically ranges based on program selection, medical needs, and location. Our surrogacy costs are transparent and include all necessary services. We offer detailed cost breakdowns and payment plans to help make surrogacy accessible. Contact us for a personalized estimate based on your specific situation.

question3.q: How much does it cost to do surrogacy with IVF?
question3.a: The total cost for surrogacy with IVF includes IVF procedures, embryo transfer, surrogate mother compensation, legal services, and ongoing medical support. Surrogacy costs are comprehensive and cover all aspects from initial consultation through birth. We provide itemized pricing and can discuss financing options to help manage surrogacy expenses.

eggFreezing.title: Egg Freezing / Preservation
eggFreezing.description: Egg freezing, also known as oocyte cryopreservation, is a process that allows women to preserve their fertility by freezing their eggs for future use. This advanced reproductive technology offers women the opportunity to delay childbearing while maintaining the quality of their eggs.

process.title: The Egg Freezing Process
process.step1: The process begins with ovarian stimulation using fertility medications to encourage the development of multiple eggs. This is carefully monitored through blood tests and ultrasounds to ensure optimal egg development.
process.step2: Once the eggs reach maturity, they are retrieved through a minor surgical procedure called egg retrieval. The procedure is performed under sedation and typically takes 15-20 minutes.
process.step3: The retrieved eggs are immediately frozen using a process called vitrification, which preserves them at extremely low temperatures. This advanced freezing technique maintains egg quality and viability for future use.

benefits.title: Benefits of Egg Freezing
benefits.step1: Preserve fertility for women who want to delay childbearing due to career, education, or personal reasons. This is especially valuable for women in their late 20s to early 30s.
benefits.step2: Maintain egg quality by freezing eggs when they are at their peak fertility. This can be particularly beneficial for women facing medical treatments that may affect fertility.
benefits.step3: Provide peace of mind and reproductive options for the future. Frozen eggs can be stored for many years and used when the time is right for starting a family.

question1.q: What is the cost of egg freezing and preservation?
question1.a: The cost of egg freezing includes ovarian stimulation medications, egg retrieval procedure, vitrification process, and annual storage fees. Egg freezing costs vary based on the number of cycles needed and storage duration. We provide transparent pricing and can discuss payment plans to make egg preservation accessible.

question2.q: How much does it cost to freeze eggs for future IVF?
question2.a: Egg freezing costs for future IVF use include the stimulation cycle, retrieval procedure, freezing process, and storage. The total cost depends on your specific needs and how many eggs are retrieved. We offer comprehensive packages and can provide detailed cost breakdowns during your consultation.

question3.q: What are the costs associated with egg preservation?
question3.a: Egg preservation costs include medical procedures, medications, laboratory fees, and ongoing storage. The initial cost covers the complete freezing process, while annual storage fees apply for long-term preservation. We provide transparent pricing and can discuss all costs associated with egg freezing during your initial consultation.

surrogacyWithEggDonor.title: Surrogacy with Egg Donor
surrogacyWithEggDonor.description: For intended parents who need donor eggs, our surrogacy with egg donor program provides a comprehensive solution. This program combines the expertise of carefully screened egg donors with our experienced surrogates to help you build your family.

process.title: The Process
process.step1: We carefully match you with a qualified egg donor who meets your specific criteria. Our donors undergo thorough medical, psychological, and genetic screening to ensure the highest quality standards.
process.step2: The selected egg donor undergoes ovarian stimulation and egg retrieval. The eggs are then fertilized with the intended father's sperm or donor sperm through IVF procedures.
process.step3: The resulting embryos are transferred to a carefully matched surrogate who carries the pregnancy to term. Throughout the process, we provide comprehensive support and monitoring for all parties involved.

benefits.title: Benefits of This Program
benefits.step1: Access to a diverse pool of carefully screened egg donors with detailed profiles including medical history, education, and personal characteristics.
benefits.step2: Comprehensive support throughout the entire process, from donor selection to birth, ensuring a smooth and successful journey to parenthood.
benefits.step3: High success rates with our experienced medical team and proven protocols, giving you the best chance of achieving a successful pregnancy and healthy baby.

question1.q: What is the surrogacy price with egg donor?
question1.a: The gestational surrogacy price with egg donor includes donor compensation, IVF procedures, surrogate mother pay, legal services, and comprehensive medical support. Our surrogacy cost breakdown is transparent and covers all expenses from donor selection through birth. The price for surrogate mother services varies based on program specifics and location.

question2.q: How much does surrogacy cost when using an egg donor?
question2.a: Surrogacy costs with egg donor typically include donor fees, medical procedures, surrogate compensation, and legal expenses. The cost for surrogacy varies based on your specific needs and program selection. We provide detailed cost breakdowns and transparent pricing to help you understand all surrogacy expenses involved in the process.

question3.q: How much does it cost for surrogacy with donor eggs?
question3.a: The total cost of surrogacy with donor eggs includes egg donor compensation, IVF and embryo transfer procedures, surrogate mother price, legal documentation, and ongoing support. Surrogacy costs are comprehensive and we offer transparent pricing with detailed breakdowns. Contact us for a personalized estimate based on your specific requirements.

vipConciergeServices.title: VIP Concierge Services
vipConciergeServices.description: Our VIP Concierge Services provide comprehensive support for international intended parents, offering housing, translation, transportation, and personalized assistance throughout your surrogacy journey. We ensure your comfort and peace of mind during this important time.

services.title: Our VIP Services
services.step1: Premium housing accommodations in comfortable, fully-furnished apartments or hotels near medical facilities. We arrange everything from booking to check-in, ensuring your stay is comfortable and convenient.
services.step2: Professional translation services for all medical appointments, legal meetings, and important communications. Our certified translators ensure clear communication throughout your journey.
services.step3: Comprehensive transportation services including airport transfers, medical appointment transportation, and local travel assistance. We provide reliable, comfortable transportation for all your needs.

benefits.title: Why Choose VIP Services
benefits.step1: Seamless experience with all logistics handled by our experienced team, allowing you to focus on your journey to parenthood without worrying about practical details.
benefits.step2: Cultural and language support to help you navigate the local environment comfortably, with assistance available 24/7 for any questions or concerns.
benefits.step3: Peace of mind knowing that every aspect of your stay is professionally managed, from accommodation to transportation, ensuring a stress-free experience during this important time.

question1.q: What is included in VIP concierge services for surrogacy?
question1.a: Our VIP concierge services include premium accommodations, professional translation for all medical and legal appointments, comprehensive transportation including airport transfers, personal coordination, and 24/7 support. These services complement our surrogacy programs and ensure a seamless experience throughout your journey, regardless of surrogacy costs.

question2.q: How much does VIP concierge service cost for surrogacy?
question2.a: VIP concierge service costs vary based on the duration of stay, accommodation preferences, and level of support needed. The price includes all concierge services and is separate from surrogacy costs. We provide transparent pricing and can customize packages based on your specific needs and surrogacy program requirements.

question3.q: Do VIP services affect the overall surrogacy cost?
question3.a: VIP concierge services are optional and priced separately from core surrogacy costs. While surrogacy costs cover medical and legal services, VIP services provide additional comfort and convenience. We offer flexible packages and can discuss how VIP services can enhance your surrogacy experience without significantly impacting total surrogacy expenses.

hero.title: Gestational Surrogacy in Georgia: Your Secure Path to Parenthood
hero.description: Embark on a legally protected, world-class surrogacy journey in the Republic of Georgia. Since 2011, Georgia has offered one of the world's most supportive legal frameworks for international intended parents, granting automatic parental rights from birth with no court orders or adoption required. Our comprehensive, compassionate programs combine state-of-the-art IVF clinics, fully screened surrogates, and complete legal support to help you create your happy family safely and affordably.

highlights.title: Immediate Legal Parentage
highlights.description: Intended parents' names go directly on the birth certificate at birth.

highlights.title: 70% Savings vs. United States
highlights.description: All-inclusive, transparent packages starting from $40,000 to $60,000.

highlights.title: Zero Surrogate Parental Rights
highlights.description: The surrogate mother has no legal right to be recognized as a parent.

highlights.title: World-Class IVF Clinics
highlights.description: State-of-the-art medical equipment and high clinical success rates.

highlights.title: Fast and Secure Exit Process
highlights.description: Administrative birth certificate registration in just 5 to 10 days.

benefits.title: Why Intended Parents Choose Georgia Country
benefits.subtitle: Discover why thousands of couples from the UK, Europe, Australia, and the US choose Georgia as their trusted surrogacy destination.

cards.title: 100% Legally Secure
cards.description: Georgian law has explicitly protected intended parents since 2011. Under Article 143 of the Law on Health Care, you are recognized as the sole legal parents on the birth certificate from birth. The surrogate mother has zero legal or parental rights to the child, ensuring a worry-free process.

cards.title: Affordable Excellence
cards.description: Save up to 70% compared to surrogacy programs in the United States. Our all-inclusive, transparent packages cover top-tier IVF clinics, surrogate compensation, comprehensive prenatal care, and delivery in modern maternity hospitals with absolutely zero hidden fees.

cards.title: Streamlined Exit Process
cards.description: No pre-parentage court orders, special committee approvals, or post-birth adoption proceedings are required. Once the birth certificate is issued by the Civil Registry (usually within 5-10 days of birth), you can immediately apply for your baby's passport and travel home safely.

legal.title: The Legal Framework: 100% Protected Rights
legal.subtitle: A clear, regulated, and supportive legal framework ensures complete peace of mind for international intended parents.
legal.overview: The legal framework governing assisted reproductive technologies (ART) in Georgia is exceptionally clear and favorable for international intended parents. Under Article 143 of the Law of Georgia on Health Care (enacted in 2011), gestational surrogacy and egg/sperm donation are fully legal, regulated, and supported by the state.
legal.protectionsTitle: Key Legal Protections under Georgian Law:

protections.title: Automatic Legal Parentage
protections.description: Intended parents are recognized as the sole, automatic legal parents of the child from the moment of conception. No court orders, special committee approvals, or post-birth adoption proceedings are required.

protections.title: No Rights for Surrogate Mother
protections.description: Under Article 143, the surrogate mother (and any egg/sperm donor) has explicitly zero parental rights to the child. The surrogate mother cannot be recognized as a parent and has no legal right to keep the child after birth.

protections.title: Direct Birth Certificate Registration
protections.description: Your names are placed directly on the child's birth certificate as the mother and father. There is absolutely no mention of surrogacy, egg donation, or the surrogate mother on the official document.

requirements.title: Heterosexual Couples Only
requirements.description: Under current Georgian statutes, surrogacy and IVF programs are exclusively available to heterosexual couples. Single individuals and same-sex couples are not eligible.

requirements.title: Married or Cohabiting
requirements.description: Couples must be legally married or cohabiting for at least one year. A marriage certificate or cohabitation agreement must be provided.

requirements.title: Medical Need for Surrogacy
requirements.description: Intended parents must provide medical documentation of infertility, repeated IVF failures, anatomical uterine issues, or other medical reasons that make carrying a pregnancy impossible or dangerous.

process.title: The Gestational Surrogacy Process
process.subtitle: A comprehensive, step-by-step guide to your surrogacy journey in Georgia.

steps.title: Initial Consultation & Matching
steps.description: Your journey begins with a detailed consultation to discuss your needs, review medical history, and explain the programs. We then match you with a fully-screened surrogate mother and/or egg donor from our extensive database. All surrogates undergo rigorous medical, psychological, and background checks.

steps.title: Legal & Administrative Setup
steps.description: Drafting and signing of clear legal contracts that secure your parental rights and outline the responsibilities of all parties. These contracts are prepared in accordance with Georgian law and are notarized. We also assist with translating and preparing all required documents.

steps.title: IVF & Embryo Transfer
steps.description: The IVF procedure is performed at a state-of-the-art clinic in Tbilisi. Embryos are created using the intended parents' gametes or donor gametes, and are then transferred to the surrogate mother. Any remaining high-quality embryos are cryopreserved for future use.

steps.title: Pregnancy Care & Monitoring
steps.description: Once pregnancy is confirmed, the surrogate mother receives comprehensive prenatal care, regular medical checkups, and emotional support. We provide you with constant medical updates, ultrasound reports, and video calls, ensuring you are fully involved in every milestone of the pregnancy.

steps.title: Birth & Streamlined Exit Process
steps.description: The baby is delivered in a modern maternity hospital in Tbilisi. Following birth, the Civil Registry issues the official birth certificate within 5-10 days, naming only the intended parents. We then assist you with preparing the documentation for your home country's embassy to secure your baby's passport and travel home safely.

pricing.title: Transparent & Affordable Costs
pricing.subtitle: Save up to 70% compared to US surrogacy with complete financial transparency and guaranteed programs.
pricing.overview: Gestational surrogacy in Georgia is exceptionally cost-effective, with all-inclusive programs typically ranging from $40,000 to $60,000 USD. This represents a fraction of the cost of similar programs in the United States or Canada, where expenses can easily exceed $150,000. Georgia's lower cost of living and highly competitive medical sector allow us to offer world-class medical care and generous surrogate compensation at an affordable price.
pricing.inclusionsTitle: What is Included in Our All-Inclusive Packages?

inclusions.title: IVF & Medical Procedures
inclusions.description: All clinical fees, embryo creation, ICSI, PGT-A testing (if selected), and embryo transfers.

inclusions.title: Surrogate Compensation & Care
inclusions.description: Generous monthly allowance, maternity clothing, housing support, and post-birth compensation.

inclusions.title: Legal & Administrative Support
inclusions.description: Contract drafting, notary fees, translation, birth registration, and comprehensive embassy exit support.

inclusions.title: Prenatal Care & Maternity Delivery
inclusions.description: All prenatal checkups, medications, ultrasounds, and delivery in a premium private maternity hospital.

features: Full legal contract and birth registration support

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find detailed answers to the most common questions about surrogacy in Georgia country.

items.question: Is surrogacy legal in Georgia for foreign couples?
items.answer: Yes, gestational surrogacy and egg/sperm donation have been 100% legal, regulated, and supported by Georgian law since 2011. Under national statutes, the surrogate mother has explicitly zero parental rights, and intended parents are registered directly on the birth certificate as the sole legal parents of the child.

items.question: Who is eligible to pursue surrogacy in Georgia?
items.answer: Under current laws, surrogacy is available exclusively to heterosexual couples who are legally married or cohabiting for at least one year. Intended parents must also provide medical documentation of infertility or an inability to carry a pregnancy safely.

items.question: What is the status of the proposed 2023 draft law?
items.answer: In June 2023, the Georgian government proposed a draft bill to restrict commercial surrogacy to Georgian citizens. However, this bill has not been passed, and its discussion has been repeatedly postponed (most recently in mid-2025/2026). Surrogacy for foreign intended parents remains fully legal, operational, and safe under the existing legal framework.

items.question: How does the parentage and exit process work?
items.answer: The intended parents acquire legal parentage automatically at birth. Within 5-10 days of birth, the Civil Registry issues the child's birth certificate naming only the intended parents. You can then immediately apply for your baby's passport at your home country's embassy in Tbilisi to travel home safely.
